cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Zeta Global research: AI agents now control discovery and evaluation before shoppers reach brand sites
stories.plain: Zeta Global's survey finds consumers are authorizing AI agents to shop and buy on their behalf, with 70% still preferring to complete transactions on brand-owned channels. The critical shift: AI agents now own the discovery and evaluation stage, meaning brands that aren't visible inside AI answers may never get considered. This redraws how marketing budgets and channel strategies should be built.

stories.head: Bad conversion data is training ad bidding algorithms to optimize for the wrong customers
stories.plain: When conversion data fed into automated ad platforms is inaccurate, the bidding AI learns from the wrong signals and keeps repeating the mistake at scale. This is not a reporting problem — it's a model-poisoning problem that compounds over time. Marketers need clean, verified conversion inputs or automated bidding actively works against them.

stories.head: AI platforms are making creative — not audience targeting — the primary signal for ad delivery
stories.plain: Google, Meta, and TikTok are automating audience selection, which shifts the marketer's real lever to creative quality as a targeting signal. Headlines, images, and video now determine who sees an ad more than demographic picks do. This fundamentally changes how creative teams are resourced and how campaigns are measured.

stories.head: Zenity Labs: attackers are hijacking enterprise AI infrastructure via LiteLLM vulnerabilities
stories.plain: New Zenity Labs research shows threat actors exploiting critical vulnerabilities in LiteLLM — a widely used AI gateway — to hijack enterprise AI infrastructure and run attacks against third parties. For marketing teams deploying AI pipelines in production, this is a direct governance and security risk that procurement and IT need to address before rollout.

stories.head: Optimizely study of 2,000+ B2B marketers: AI adoption is creating more work, not more time
stories.plain: A global survey across seven markets finds that current AI rollouts often fragment workflows and increase manual intervention rather than cutting effort. Brand consistency is also suffering. The findings challenge vendor promises of instant efficiency and point to a systemic integration gap that leaders need to solve before AI delivers strategic value.

stories.head: Zeta Global research: AI agents now control discovery before shoppers reach brand sites
stories.plain: New Zeta Global data shows 70% of AI-assisted shoppers still complete purchases on brand-owned channels, but AI agents are already deciding which brands get considered — before any brand touchpoint. This shifts the battle for customers to the discovery layer, meaning brands that don't show up inside AI answers may never get a chance to convert.

stories.head: Databricks CustomerLake is restructuring how CDPs work at the data layer
stories.plain: Databricks' CustomerLake moves the unified customer profile into the data lakehouse itself, bypassing traditional CDP vendors. This matters because whoever owns the data layer increasingly owns the intelligence layer — and agentic systems need a single, live source of customer truth to make real-time decisions. A structural shift, not a feature update.

stories.head: Agentic AI is breaking martech pricing models — one afternoon of tool-calling can consume a monthly subscription
stories.plain: When AI agents call multiple tools repeatedly to complete a task, per-seat SaaS pricing collapses as a model and data architecture becomes the cost driver. The article argues the fix is keeping data closer to where agents act, not limiting tool use. Directly changes how buyers should evaluate and budget for agentic platforms.

stories.head: Bad conversion data is actively training ad bidding algorithms to optimize for the wrong customers
stories.plain: In automated ad platforms, inaccurate conversion signals don't just distort reports — they become the training signal for bidding AI, compounding errors over time. The piece explains why data quality is now a governance and budget risk, not just a measurement hygiene issue, in any AI-driven paid media program.

stories.head: Optimizely survey of 2,000+ B2B marketers finds AI is creating more work, not less, due to fragmented integration
stories.plain: A large-scale global study finds that current AI adoption often increases manual intervention and fragments workflows rather than freeing up time. For buyers evaluating AI tools, this is a red flag about point-solution sprawl and underscores why governance and integration strategy must come before tool selection.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: Agentic AI is rewiring the economics of the entire martech stack
body: AI agents are no longer a feature bolted onto existing tools — they are beginning to replace the connective tissue between systems, forcing a rethink of what a martech stack even is. Zeta Global research confirms buyers are moving toward agentic commerce, where AI acts on their behalf before a human ever touches a decision.

This shift is also destabilising the best-of-breed model: when agents orchestrate workflows across dozens of point solutions, the integration overhead becomes unmanageable and vendors with unified platforms gain a structural edge. Marketing operations roles are next in line for redefinition — or elimination.

themes.head: Garbage in, wasted budget out — data quality becomes the AI bottleneck
body: Every AI optimisation loop amplifies whatever data it trains on, which means bad CRM records, mis-labelled audiences, and stale signals now destroy ad budgets faster than they did in the rules-based era. The Databricks CustomerLake launch is a direct response: moving customer data into a governed lakehouse so AI models touch clean, unified profiles rather than fragmented warehouse exports.

Synthetic data is emerging as a partial remedy — letting teams simulate customer behaviour to fill gaps — but it introduces its own validity risks. The underlying decision is the same: data infrastructure is now an AI readiness question, not just a compliance or IT one.

themes.head: Creative becomes targeting — personalization shifts from audience to asset
body: As cookie-based audience segments erode, AI is enabling a different lever: generating thousands of creative variants tuned to context, channel, and moment so that the ad itself does the targeting work audiences once did. Flodesk Studio and the Runware / Gemini Omni Flash API launch both illustrate how generative image and copy tools are becoming native to campaign workflows rather than separate production steps.

Digital Asset Management is the infrastructure consequence — DAM systems must now version, tag, and serve AI-generated assets at scale, making them a strategic layer rather than a storage utility. The risk is volume without strategy: more content created faster does not automatically mean more relevant content.

themes.head: AI delivers more work, not more time — the productivity paradox bites marketers
body: Optimizely research and multiple practitioner voices this month converge on an uncomfortable finding: AI tools are expanding the scope of what teams are asked to do rather than shrinking their hours. Faster content drafts create pressure to publish more; faster analysis surfaces more decisions that need human judgment.

The strategic response emerging from leaders like LiveRamp's Daniella Harkins is deliberate AI portfolio management — auditing which use cases genuinely return time versus which just generate noise. Cutting through hype requires accepting that most AI initiatives will fail to move the needle before finding the ones that do.
ev: Agile Brand · More work not time

themes.head: Search & discovery are breaking the ranking contract with brands
body: AI-generated answers in search are absorbing clicks that used to flow to #1 organic results, decoupling visibility from traffic in a way that invalidates years of SEO investment logic. Brands ranking at the top are discovering that impressions climb while sessions and conversions stall — a measurement crisis as much as a channel one.

The implication for strategy is that owned content must now be optimised for AI citation and summarisation, not just keyword match, and that email and direct channels regain importance as traffic from search becomes less reliable. This reshapes budget allocation decisions across the entire acquisition funnel.

month.takeaway: Across every layer of the stack — data, creative, search, workflow, and measurement — AI is simultaneously creating new leverage and exposing the organisational debt that will decide who captures it.
